steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
The passage establishes how achiral molecules behave when flipped (yields identical molecule), then shifts to chiral molecules.

The key signal is “rather than an identical one” — this phrase emphasizes the contrast: chiral molecules produce inverted molecules, not identical ones like achiral molecules.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “By contrast,” is the only choice that explicitly marks a shift between opposing behaviors. The other three either suggest restatement, exemplification, or similarity — none appropriate for contrasting two different processes. That matches Choice B — by contrast,.
